meta.operator: Capital Regional District (CRD) Integrated Water Services
data_licences: Open Government Licence – Canada (Environment and Climate Change Canada)

source.primary: Sooke Lake Reservoir (primary), Goldstream River (secondary), Thetis Lake (tertiary)
source.wfi_note: Sooke watershed adjacent to fire-prone terrain on Vancouver Island; CRD owns entire watershed

sooke_lake.name: Sooke Lake Reservoir
sooke_lake.data_note: ~2-year supply at current demand; CRD owns entire watershed

storage.per_capita_dam3_note: Based on usable capacity / population served
storage.data_note: CRD does not publish real-time reservoir levels; values from CRD annual report

plants.name: CRD Drinking Water Treatment Plant
plants.process: UV disinfection, chlorination (no filtration — closed watershed)

treatment.fluoride_note: Fluoride removed in 2011 by CRD board vote

restriction.hypothesized_triggers: CRD activates Stage 1 when reservoir approaches ~70% of capacity heading into summer

risk.single_point_of_failure: Sooke Flowline (44km concrete aqueduct, built 1915) — aging primary conveyance
risk.climate_note: Below-average snowpack; 2-year reservoir buffer provides resilience. Sooke Flowline age (~110 years) is a growing concern.

use.data_note: Sector split from CRD annual report

egress.cso_note: Victoria controversially discharged raw sewage until 2020; McLoughlin Point upgrade completed that year

sooke_upper.station_name: Sooke River upstream of Charters Creek
